preamble: Primary palatalization of velars involves a significant forward shift in place of articulation. A palatalized voiceless velar stop [kʲ] moves forward to the postalveolar region and develops a fricative release, resulting in the affricate [tʃ].

examples.note: The First Slavic Palatalization changed velars to postalveolar affricates/fricatives before front vowels.
Latin /k/ before front vowels /e, i/ regularly developed into [tʃ] in Italian.
Old English underwent palatalization of velars near front vowels, shifting [k] to [tʃ].

sources: Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
